elements.content:
Request Enters the Stack

A user question hits the gateway, then moves into the API layer to start the flow.

- User sends a question
- Gateway receives it
- API layer starts processing

Prompt Assembly

The API combines instructions and the user message into one conversation package.

- System context
- User message
- Single assembled prompt

Inference Happens

Claude reads the assembled prompt, predicts tokens, and streams the answer back.

- Prompt enters the model
- Inference generates tokens
- Tokens become the reply

Response Comes Back

The API shapes the model output into a clean response, then the gateway returns it to the user.

- Model reply returns
- API formats response
- Gateway sends it back

Debug These Three First

When Claude feels broken, start with the boring parts of the pipeline.

- API Layer: auth, rate limits, request shape
- Prompt Assembly: missing context or bad instructions
- Model Latency: slow inference or overloaded calls
